name: companion
title: Companion
description: Manage local SKILL.md packages with Companion: self-update first, validate, publish, update, resolve dependencies, track installs, and repair manifests.
notes:
## What it does

The Companion skill gives your assistant one reliable workflow for local SKILL.md packages: validate, publish, update, organize, install, audit, and self-update.

## Safety model

- Reads only skill folders you point it at and Companion's own local state under ~/.companion.
- Stores credentials in ~/.companion/credentials.json and never prints tokens.
- Confirms publishing or archival changes before mutating workspace skills.
- Uses companion.json as the source of truth for package identity, version, env/secrets, dependencies, notes, commands, and changelog.

## Local tracking

Companion records installed skills in ~/.companion/skills.lock.json so audits can quickly compare local versions, checksums, and dependency snapshots against the workspace registry.

changes: Moves Companion registry identity and version out of SKILL.md metadata and into companion.json.
Adds manifest v2 with markdown notes, env/secrets declarations, dependency name-to-id mapping, commands, and changelog metadata.
Adds local install tracking guidance for ~/.companion/skills.lock.json.
Runs the mandatory Companion self-update check once per conversation instead of before every Companion turn.
Reports org skills published through Companion as installed for the current user.
Runs a mandatory Companion self-update check at the start of every Companion invocation before validating, publishing, updating, labeling, or installing any other skill.

Installs newer bundled Companion versions through /local-skills/companion/package, verifies the staged package, backs up the old folder, reports the installed version, and then asks the user to rerun when runtime reload is not guaranteed.
Requires explicit publish placement for brand-new skills: Personal/My Skills vs Org/everyone, then existing folder, new folder, or no folder before POST /skills.
Documents scope as valid on first publish only, with updates preserving existing scope and labels unless the user explicitly adds folders.

COMPANION_API_URL.description: Workspace API base URL. The install/use prompt stores this in ~/.companion/credentials.json when the environment variable is not set.

COMPANION_TOKEN.description: Personal access token scoped to skills:read and skills:write. The install/use prompt stores this in ~/.companion/credentials.json when the environment variable is not set.

commands.name: Publish a skill
commands.desc: Validate a skill, ask Personal vs Org and folder placement, then publish it safely.

commands.name: Update a skill
commands.desc: Push a new version with targeted identity checks.

commands.name: Organize with labels
commands.desc: Create, assign, rename, recolor, or delete org and personal folder labels.

commands.name: Resolve dependencies
commands.desc: Analyze packages and sync companion.json before upload.

commands.name: Sync companion.json
commands.desc: Create or repair manifest v2 with identity, env/secrets, dependency ids, notes, commands, and changelog.

commands.name: Manage skill API calls
commands.desc: Use the supported skills API surface without crossing into workspace admin.

commands.name: Update Companion skill
commands.desc: Check and install the latest bundled Companion skill safely.

commands.name: Check for updates
commands.desc: Compare installed skills and dependencies against the workspace registry.

commands.name: Install updates
commands.desc: Bring outdated skills up to the latest published version.

commands.name: Validate a skill
commands.desc: Check a skill is well formed before you share it.

commands.name: Manage your skills
commands.desc: List and organize the skills on this machine.
